title: Luxury Ad Shot Generator
summary: Premium campaign-style image prompt for products, launches, and brand visuals.
use_when: Use when you need one polished hero image with commercial clarity and strong material realism.
do_not_use_when: Do not use when you want messy collage energy, documentary realism, or fast broad ideation.
proof_note: Stronger outputs come from defining the shot, not just the subject.
common_failure: The result looks generic and stock-photo-like.
failure_why: There is no visual tension, no camera language, and no exclusion list.
quick_patch: Add a contrast pair, a lens/framing note, a texture emphasis, and explicit exclusions.

compare.weak: Make a cool luxury ad for a perfume bottle.
compare.improved: Create one premium ad shot for a matte black perfume bottle on wet basalt, minimal luxury fused with raw cinematic imperfection, hard flash plus amber rim light, macro lens, controlled reflections, dense condensation, charcoal / silver / amber palette, clean silhouette, negative space for copy, avoid clutter and stock-photo feel.
why_better: Defines the shot, not just the object
Adds tension: minimal luxury + raw imperfection

example.input: Product: Nocturne fragrance bottle, matte black glass, mysterious + elite mood.
example.output: Macro ad image of a matte black fragrance bottle on wet basalt, amber rim glow, silver reflections, dense condensation, campaign-grade polish, clean negative space.

variants.Universal: Create one premium campaign image for [SUBJECT] with clear focal hierarchy, strong light behaviour, material realism, a disciplined palette, and strict exclusions for clutter or muddy lighting. Return prompt + negative prompt + caption.
variants.Claude: Act as a disciplined creative director. Prioritise coherence, restraint, and hierarchy over style overload. Define scene, composition, light, lens, material realism, palette, and exclusions. Return final prompt, negative prompt, and caption.
variants.Copilot: Generate a production-ready image prompt for [SUBJECT] in a premium ad style. Include subject, scene, composition, lighting, lens/framing, material textures, colour palette, and exclusions. Return prompt, negative prompt, and caption.
variants.Gemini: Create a visually cinematic image prompt for [SUBJECT] with strong spatial and sensory detail. Define scene layout, focal hierarchy, camera perspective, light behaviour, tactile textures, palette, and intended emotional impact. Return prompt, negative prompt, and caption.

title: Thumbnail That Demands a Click
summary: High-CTR thumbnail concept prompt for YouTube, reels, and social covers.
use_when: Use when one-second readability matters more than visual nuance.
do_not_use_when: Do not use when the goal is subtle branding or complex artistic storytelling.
proof_note: High click-through visuals win through clarity and reaction, not detail density.
common_failure: The thumbnail is crowded and visually confusing.
failure_why: There is no dominant subject and the concept asks for too many visual ideas at once.
quick_patch: Reduce to one subject, one emotion, one visual question, and preserve text space.

compare.weak: Make a viral YouTube thumbnail about AI.
compare.improved: Create a high-CTR thumbnail for a video about AI replacing jobs: one shocked face lit by screen glow, one red warning graph reflection in glasses, dark blurred office behind, strong contrast, left-side negative space for title, visual story readable in 1 second.
why_better: Adds space for actual title overlay

example.input: Topic: AI replaced my workflow in 7 days. Tone: dramatic.
example.output: One shocked face, screen glow, red graph reflection in glasses, dark office behind, strong contrast, title space on left.

variants.Universal: Create one high-CTR thumbnail concept for [TOPIC] with one dominant subject, one emotional cue, high contrast, and title-safe negative space. Return prompt, overlays, and colour direction.
variants.Claude: Design a high-CTR thumbnail for [TOPIC] with brutal clarity: one subject, one emotion, one visual question. Prioritise comprehension speed over decoration. Return prompt, overlay options, colour direction, and a short note on why the composition works.
variants.Copilot: Create a thumbnail prompt for [TOPIC] with one dominant subject, a clear emotional reaction, simple background, strong contrast, and room for text. Then output 3 short overlay phrases, colour direction, and one sentence explaining the click logic.
variants.Gemini: Generate a click-focused thumbnail concept for [TOPIC] optimised for one-second readability. Define focal subject, emotional cue, spacing for text, contrast strategy, and visual tension. Return prompt, overlays, and colour direction.

title: Film-Still Character Builder
summary: Cinematic portrait prompt that hides worldbuilding and biography inside visual detail.
use_when: Use when a portrait feels flat and you need implied story, world, or status inside the frame.
do_not_use_when: Do not use when you need clean catalog portraiture or simple reference-sheet output.
proof_note: Adding hidden biography and material detail usually beats genre buzzwords.
common_failure: The portrait looks like generic cyberpunk/fantasy art.
failure_why: It relies on surface descriptors instead of posture, wear, prop logic, and atmosphere.
quick_patch: Add one hidden history detail, one lighting source, one lens choice, and one texture note.

compare.weak: Create a cool cyberpunk woman portrait.
compare.improved: Create a prestige-film portrait of a late-30s courier in a rain-soaked retro-futurist city, stoic expression, worn leather collar, faint scar under left eye, projector-blue spill on cheekbone, 85mm close-up, visible skin texture, wet fabric fibers, restrained neon, hidden history implied by patched insignia.
why_better: Replaces genre cliché with specific biography clues
Uses texture to imply realism and budget

example.input: Character: former underground courier turned resistance leader.
example.output: Close 85mm portrait, projector-blue spill, weathered leather collar, patched insignia, rain haze, scar detail, brutalist blur behind.

variants.Universal: Create one prestige-film portrait for [CHARACTER IDEA] with implied biography, strong framing, material detail, one defining signature clue, and specific light behaviour. Return hero, darker, and cleaner versions.
variants.Claude: Create a cinematic portrait prompt for [CHARACTER IDEA] with implied biography and world logic. Focus on posture, expression, signature detail, material wear, and atmosphere. Keep it restrained and emotionally loaded. Return hero, darker, and cleaner versions plus one line on what the image implies.
variants.Copilot: Write a detailed portrait prompt for [CHARACTER IDEA]. Include appearance, clothing wear, props, posture, mood, world style, lens/framing, lighting, and texture cues. Keep it cinematic and story-rich, then output a main prompt plus two alternate refinements.
variants.Gemini: Generate a visually rich portrait prompt for [CHARACTER IDEA], emphasizing framing, light behavior, tactile realism, and emotional tone. Add one darker variation and one cleaner editorial variation.

title: Executive Email Rewriter
summary: Turns rough, emotional, or messy drafts into calm, action-ready professional emails.
use_when: Use when a message is too long, defensive, unclear, or emotionally noisy.
do_not_use_when: Do not use when you still have not decided what you actually need from the recipient.
proof_note: A strong rewrite preserves the ask while removing emotional drag and ambiguity.
common_failure: The rewrite sounds polished but the real request becomes vague.
failure_why: The prompt optimizes tone but not structure or action clarity.
quick_patch: Force the structure: context → key point → action requested → close.

compare.improved: Rewrite this draft into a calm, concise, professional email. Preserve the actual request, remove repetition and defensiveness, structure it as context -> key point -> action requested -> close if useful, and return polished + shorter versions with 3 subject lines.
why_better: Defines structure instead of style only

example.input: Draft complaining that the order list is still not updated and asking for confirmation by tomorrow.
example.output: Context: the order list still misses the updated quantities. Key point: final confirmation is needed to avoid delays. Action: please confirm the final list by tomorrow 12:00.

variants.Universal: Rewrite the message below into a clear, professional email that sounds calm, competent, and easy to act on. Preserve intent, remove repetition, filler, defensiveness, emotional spillover, and vagueness, make the ask obvious, and keep the tone confident but not cold. Return polished version + shorter version + 3 subject lines.
variants.Claude: Rewrite the following message into a calm, competent, action-oriented email. Preserve intent, remove defensiveness and filler, and make the requested action explicit. Return polished, shorter, and ultra-short versions plus 3 subject lines.
variants.Copilot: Rewrite this email draft to be professional, concise, and easy to act on. Keep the intent, remove filler and emotional language, structure the message clearly, and provide 3 subject line options.
variants.Gemini: Transform the draft into a concise professional email with clear action, better tone, and minimal ambiguity. Return a polished version, a shorter version, and three subject line choices.

title: Deep Research Synthesizer
summary: Decision-oriented synthesis prompt for briefings, trade-offs, and recommendations.
use_when: Use when you need a serious briefing with knowns, unknowns, trade-offs, and an explicit recommendation.
do_not_use_when: Do not use when you only want a quick shallow summary or broad brainstorming list.
proof_note: Research gets more trustworthy when facts, assumptions, and uncertainty are separated.
common_failure: The result becomes polished trivia instead of a decision memo.
failure_why: The prompt asks for summary but never asks for question structure, trade-offs, or confidence levels.
quick_patch: Force the core question, subquestions, knowns, unknowns, trade-offs, and recommendation sections.

compare.improved: Build a decision-oriented research synthesis about [topic]. Define the core question, break it into 5-7 subquestions, separate high-confidence findings from contested points, map trade-offs, explain implications for [context], and end with a concise recommendation plus confidence note.
why_better: Frames the work around decisions, not trivia
Produces output you can actually use

example.input: Question: Should a team standardize on one LLM or use multiple?
example.output: High confidence: different models vary by strength. Trade-off: simplicity vs specialization. Recommendation: use one primary standard with defined exceptions by task.

variants.Universal: Build a decision-oriented research synthesis on [TOPIC / QUESTION]. Return: core question, subquestions, knowns, unknowns, trade-offs, implications, recommendation, and confidence note. Separate facts, assumptions, and interpretation.
variants.Claude: Create a research memo on [TOPIC] optimized for decision-making. First define the core question and subquestions, then separate high-confidence findings, contested points, trade-offs, and implications for [CONTEXT]. End with a recommendation and a confidence note.
variants.Copilot: Write a structured research synthesis about [TOPIC] with sections for core question, subquestions, known facts, uncertainties, trade-offs, implications, and recommendation. Include a comparison table if relevant.
variants.Gemini: Generate a decision-oriented research brief on [TOPIC] with clear sections for knowns, unknowns, trade-offs, implications, and final recommendation. Keep facts, assumptions, and interpretation separate.

title: Workflow Optimizer
summary: Operational prompt for diagnosing broken processes and designing lower-friction systems.
use_when: Use for recurring issues like unclear ownership, checklist failures, handover problems, order-list confusion, and rework.
do_not_use_when: Do not use when the process is still too vague to describe or when the problem is interpersonal rather than structural.
proof_note: Strong ops prompts rank problems by impact instead of giving generic improvement ideas.
common_failure: The model returns vague advice like 'communicate better' or 'use a checklist.'
failure_why: The prompt does not define the current-state process, pain points, ranking logic, or output structure.
quick_patch: Describe the current workflow, demand ranked issues, and force a future-state sequence plus template fields.

compare.weak: How can I improve this process?
compare.improved: Analyze this workflow as an operations strategist. Identify bottlenecks, ambiguity, delays, duplicated effort, and preventable errors. Rank the top five issues by impact, propose a cleaner future-state process, separate quick wins from structural changes, and include ownership, timing, checklist fields, and implementation risks.
why_better: Forces prioritization instead of generic advice

example.input: Current process: multiple people update an order list by email; quantities are missed; final confirmation arrives too late; no owner for changes.
example.output: Issue 1: no single owner for final quantity validation. Future state: one shared tracker, one change owner, cutoff time, standardized confirmation template.

variants.Universal: Analyse this workflow and improve it. Identify friction points, bottlenecks, ambiguities, delays, duplicated effort, and preventable errors. Separate quick wins from structural changes and suggest a cleaner future-state workflow with ownership, timing, communication, checklist, and tracking fields. Return current-state diagnosis, top issues ranked by impact, future-state process, quick wins, template fields, and risks.
variants.Claude: Review this workflow as an operations analyst. Diagnose the current-state friction, rank the top issues, propose a cleaner step-by-step future state, and distinguish immediate fixes from structural redesign. Include ownership, timing, communication, and risk controls.
variants.Copilot: Analyze this workflow and improve it. Find bottlenecks, delays, duplicate effort, missing ownership, and preventable errors. Return diagnosis, ranked issues, improved process, quick wins, template/checklist fields, and implementation risks.
variants.Gemini: Create a future-state workflow for the process below. Identify failure points, rank issues by impact, improve the sequence, define ownership/timing/tracking, and suggest quick wins plus risk controls.
